reasoning: Viswanathan Anand is semi-retired from classical chess and his rating of 2743 is mostly stationary. On the Oct 2025 FIDE list, he is ranked 13th. His ranking will remain ≤ 13 ('as high or higher') only as long as no players below him overtake 2743, or if those above him drop below it. The players immediately below him are Mamedyarov (2742, largely inactive), Niemann (2738), Dominguez (2738), MVL (2737), and Ding (2738). The US Chess Championship took place in October; Niemann appears to have struggled, likely dropping rating points, and Dominguez didn't play. Mamedyarov is inactive. This heavily suggests Anand will hold onto 13th place (or move up if anyone above drops) in the November FIDE ranking list, covering the first two dates. However, the January 2026 date relies on matches all the way through December 2025. Because there are several highly active players bunched just 5-6 points below his rating and fewer active players within a close range above him, the natural drift over several months favors one of them having a good tournament and bumping Anand to 14th or lower. Therefore, probability dips for the later horizon.
